descriptionParagraphs: A fish for everyday surf and travel.

The Sunstone is the foundation of Asher Pacey's quiver. This is the purest form of a classic fish that we make, an ode to Southern California. It has traditional fish roots that have been refined to perform and feel more alive.

Surfed with a classic approach, the board is doing all the work, trimming and generating speed with fast and fluid carves. Paddles and gets up to speed regardless of conditions, bringing small surf to life with easy glide and release, while also offering surprising hold in better conditions. A standard keel gives it the most classic feel, but a modern keel or upright fin can bring out another level of performance.
Bottom contour: A subtle double-concave under the back foot and vee off the exit, the Sunstone combines tradition and progression.
Deck & rocker: A pronounced beak nose to carry foam throughout the board combined with a flat rocker profile makes the Sunstone a great paddler that gets up to speed right away.
Asher and Matt's next evolution of a fish.

With the bottom contours of a performance shortboard, and a more refined rail, the Lightbender combines the best of both worlds. A flat fast rocker, combined with a balance of pivot and drive makes the lightbender a versatile board that's approachable from a performance and alternative perspective.

Flatter rocker underneath the chest for easy wave-catching with slightly more entry and exit rocker than a standard fish. Holds better in steep surf, while taking the speed/flow of a fish and mixing it with a more aggressive approach. Foam packed under the chest, easing into a modern rail and carried forward to a beak nose. Strong paddler that easily gets up to speed while continuing momentum and flow throughout the wave.

Bottom contour: Single concave under the front foot, transitioning into a slight double through the fins and vee off the tail, otherwise known as a spiral vee. The modern bottom generates speed, helps the board roll rail to rail and drive off your back foot.

Deck & rocker: Flatter rocker underneath the chest for easy wave-catching with slightly more entry and exit rocker than a fish. Holds better in steep surf, while taking the speed/flow of a fish and mixing it with a more aggressive approach. Foam packed under the chest, easing into a modern rail and carried forward to a beak nose. Strong paddler that easily gets up to speed while continuing momentum and flow throughout the wave.
Victor and Matt's take on a modern fish.

A performance fish with a bit of extra grip and speed. The bottom features a spiraling vee with a double-barrel concave set within it, which creates a great balance of speed generation and control, especially at higher speeds, while still being easy to get going. The deck carries a healthy amount of foam for solid paddle power, but the rails stay refined and performance-oriented, giving it a more modern feel despite the classic fish outline. The go-to fin option is the Fascination Twin.

Developed alongside Victor Bernardo over the past year, this model comes in two distinct versions. The blunt-nose option features a fuller outline and wider tail, designed as a fast, small-wave speed machine. The pointed-nose version leans more toward a daily-driver hybrid, striking a clean balance between fish flow and performance sensibility. Both variations are built to handle a wide range of conditions. We recommend sizing the blunt-nose similar to your Plasmic, while the pointed-nose daily driver should be sized close to a Lightbender, with a touch of added thickness.

Bottom contour: Single concave transitioning into a deep double-barrel concave that runs from the middle of the board through the tail. The deep, double-barrel concave generates effortless speed and creates lift off the back foot, while providing grip through your turns.

Deck & rocker: The Veebee packs a lot of foam, flat deck profile through to a beak nose with a low apex rail, making it a great paddler with easy rail-engagement.

A performance fish rocker that stays flat under the chest with a subtle touch of entry and exit curve. This blend delivers easy speed on the paddle-in and through turns, while still offering the pivot and responsiveness of a modern performance board.
This board will change your perspective of what a twin fin is.

The Twinsman offers the same performance as a shortboard combined with the effortless speed of a twin fin. Approachable for any style of surfing. A board that you can really push on rail or just enjoy the easy down the line speed. Paddles and gets up to speed faster than a standard performance board, but doesn't have the limitations of a traditional twin. More pivot and drive off the bottom while fitting in tighter sections. Different variations allow this concept to be applied to any wave range.

The Twinsman offers the same performance as a shortboard combined with the effortless speed of a twin fin. Approachable for any style of surfing. A board that you can really push on rail or just enjoy the easy down the line speed. Paddles and gets up to speed faster than a standard performance board, but doesn't have the limitations of a traditional twin. More pivot and drive off the bottom while fitting in tighter sections.

Bottom contour: Single concave under the front foot, transitioning into a slight double through the fins and vee off the tail, otherwise known as a spiral vee. The modern bottom generates speed, helps the board roll rail to rail and drive off your back foot.

Deck & rocker: Relaxed entry rocker lets more surface area contact the water, in turn creating better paddle, speed generation and momentum. Extra exit rocker allows the board to fit in steeper sections and be surfed more vertically than traditional twins.

Flat/concave deck with foam pushed to the perimeter, quickly tapering into a modern rail. A flat deck distributes foam evenly throughout the board and under your chest, providing more paddle and allowing the board to be ridden with extra foam.

The Insanity is a swiss army knife when it comes to high performance shortboards. Designed with versatility in mind, the Insanity has a unique ability to fit in the most critical section of the wave, while also carrying speed through flat sections with ease.

"It was a weird year where the sand was all off on the north shore and this wave insanities was breaking and it was just like a sand bottom dredging square tube and I start getting these clips coming through making these crazy steep drops just like knifing in holding it and then getting to the bottom and squaring it up"
-Matt parker

Bottom contour: Deep single concave under the front foot, transitioning into a slight double through the fins and vee off the tail, otherwise known as a spiral vee. The modern bottom generates speed, helps the board roll rail to rail and drive off your back foot.

Deck & rocker: Rocker Profile:
Relaxed entry rocker for a performance board with a subtle curve throughout and extra rocker in the exit. Relaxed entry gives more surface area under your chest for paddling and down the line speed. Extra exit rocker allows for more critical surfing by letting the board fit in steeper sections of the wave.

Deck Profile:
Hidden foam in the center and under the chest, dispersing evenly towards the beak nose and tapering quickly into a modern rail. The fuller deck profile on a performance board gives it extra paddle without sacrificing performance and allows the board to carry momentum.
Matt's newest shortboard with a twin fin flair
The Warp Twin is the perfect combination of Twin fin flair and shortboard dependability.

The narrow outline provides speed and drive, while the deeper bottom contours allow this board to be pushed without worry of sliding out. A knifey down rail tail with a sharp edge gives loads of hold and dependability.

Bottom contour: Deep single concave under the front foot, transitioning into a slight double through the fins and vee off the tail, otherwise known as a spiral vee. The modern bottom generates speed, helps the board roll rail to rail and drive off your back foot.

Deck & rocker: Rocker Profile:
Relaxed entry rocker for a performance board with a subtle curve throughout and extra rocker in the exit. Relaxed entry gives more surface area under your chest for paddling and down the line speed. Extra exit rocker allows for more critical surfing by letting the board fit in steeper sections of the wave.

Deck Profile:
Hidden foam in the center and under the chest, dispersing evenly towards the beak nose and tapering quickly into a modern rail. The fuller deck profile on a performance board gives it extra paddle without sacrificing performance and allows the board to carry momentum.
A high performance board that does more of the work for you.

Fresh off the tour, Jack Freestone was looking for something new but not out of his comfort zone. The aim of the Freewing is just that, bringing new feeling and life into a familiar design. A high performance board that does more of the work for you.

Bottom contour: Deep single concave under the front foot, transitioning into a slight double through the fins and vee off the tail, otherwise known as a spiral vee. The modern bottom generates speed, helps the board roll rail to rail and drive off your back foot.

Deck & rocker: Hidden foam in the center and under the chest, tapering quickly into a modern rail. The fuller deck profile on a performance board gives it extra paddle without sacrificing performance and allows the board to carry momentum.
A collaboration between Matt Parker and Victor Bernardo.

Album's values of speed, flow and ease injected into a more traditional high performance thruster outline. With some extra hidden foam and slight adjustments to the bottom and rail, this board allows maximum performance while maintaining speed, flow and style.

Relaxed entry rocker for a performance board with a subtle curve throughout and extra rocker in the exit. Relaxed entry gives more surface area under your chest for paddling and down the line speed. Extra exit rocker allows for more critical surfing by letting the board fit in steeper sections of the wave.

Bottom contour: Deep single concave under the front foot, transitioning into a slight double through the fins and vee off the tail, otherwise known as a spiral vee. The modern bottom generates speed, helps the board roll rail to rail and drive off your back foot.

Deck & rocker: Hidden foam in the center and under the chest, dispersing evenly towards the beak nose and tapering quickly into a modern rail. The fuller deck profile on a performance board gives it extra paddle without sacrificing performance and allows the board to carry momentum.
A collaboration between Matt Parker and Jack Freestone.

Drawing inspiration from the fin placement of our other asymmetrical designs, the ProtoAtypical blends the visual balance of a symmetrical board with the intuitive ease of an asymmetrical design. It features a performance-oriented entry and exit rocker, providing forgiving entries and smooth pivoting in tight pockets. The ProtoAtypical delivers all the advantages of a high-performance shortboard, with the added benefits of enhanced backhand responsiveness and increased drive on the forehand.

Rocker:
Relaxed entry rocker for a performance board with a subtle curve throughout and extra rocker in the exit. Relaxed entry gives more surface area under your chest for paddling and down the line speed. Extra exit rocker allows for more critical surfing by letting the board fit in steeper sections of the wave.

Bottom contour: Deep single concave under the front foot, transitioning into a slight double through the fins and vee off the tail, otherwise known as a spiral vee. The modern bottom generates speed, helps the board roll rail to rail and drive off your back foot.

Deck & rocker: Hidden foam in the center and under the chest, dispersing evenly towards the beak nose and tapering quickly into a modern rail. The fuller deck profile on a performance board gives it extra paddle without sacrificing performance and allows the board to carry momentum.
Bom Dia (Good Morning), a collaboration between Matt Parker and Victor Bernardo.

The Bom Dia is an alternative performance board that is meant to be ridden as well as you can surf. Unlike most alternative boards, the Bom Dia doesn't need much width as it generates speed efficiently and planes right off the bat. With a narrow outline and asymmetrical tail, it rolls rail to rail instantly, making it very user friendly. Strong paddler that carries speed through turns. Sized similar to an Insanity, slightly shorter with more volume than a performance board.

The Bom Dia combines the performance of a modern shortboard with the paddle and speed of an alternative board. With a narrow outline and asymmetrical tail, it rolls rail to rail instantly, while maintaining speed, making it very user friendly.

Bottom contour: Deep single concave under the front foot, transitioning into a slight double through the fins and vee off the tail, otherwise known as a spiral vee. The modern bottom generates speed, helps the board roll rail to rail and drive off your back foot.

Deck & rocker: Relaxed entry rocker for a performance board with a subtle curve throughout and extra rocker in the exit. Relaxed entry gives more surface area under your chest for paddling and down the line speed. Extra exit rocker allows for more critical surfing by letting the board fit in steeper sections of the wave.

Hidden foam in the center and under the chest, dispersing evenly towards the beak nose and tapering quickly into a modern rail. The fuller deck profile on a performance board gives it extra paddle without sacrificing performance and allows the board to carry momentum.

This is the pinnacle of our research and development of performance asymmetry over the last decade. Intuitive in design, the Disorder is intended to complement your strengths and supplement your weaknesses.
Set up for regular- or goofy-foot. Twin fin speed on your toes with quad grip and a pulled-in rail line on your heels, and now with an updated performance rocker that opens up more in smaller waves.

High performance entry rocker with extra exit rocker. The high entry rocker makes it more forgiving through turns. Extra exit rocker allows for more critical surfing by letting the board fit in steeper sections of the wave. Performance deck profile with slightly more foam under the chest. Paddles are similar to a performance board but carry momentum and speed like a twin fin.

Bottom contour: The deep single concave that rolls to a vee through the fins generates speed and allows for smooth rail-to-rail transition. Twin fin speed combined with the control and maneuverability of a quad.

Deck & rocker: High performance entry rocker with extra exit rocker. The high entry rocker makes it more forgiving through turns. Extra exit rocker allows for more critical surfing by letting the board fit in steeper sections of the wave. Performance deck profile with slightly more foam under the chest. Paddles are similar to a performance board but carry momentum and speed like a twin fin.
A collaboration between Matt Parker and Victor Bernardo.

Designed to be Victor's step-down from the Bom Dia, pulling concepts from the Vesper and applying them to the asym design of the Bom Dia. Easy speed generated from the double-barrel channel that also creates lift off the back foot, while providing grip through your turns. This paired with the efficiency and responsiveness of the asym tail/fin placement makes for effortless flow in average waves, while maintaining a high level of performance with instant rail-to-rail transitions.

Fin Placement:
Asymmetrically placed fins create an all-wheel-drive feel underfoot. The heel-side trailer is positioned near the center, aligning with the ankle hinge and tail pivot point. The toe-side boxes are straighter, closer to the rail, and more a tighter cluster, offering better backside control and enhanced frontside power.

Rocker Profile:
Flatter rocker underneath the chest for easy wave-catching with slightly more entry and exit rocker than a standard fish. Holds better in steep surf, while taking the speed/flow of a fish and mixing with a more aggressive approach.

Deck Profile:
Foam packed under the chest, easing into a modern rail and carried forward to a beak nose. Strong paddler that easily gets up to speed while continuing momentum and flow throughout the wave.

Bottom contour: Single concave transitioning into a deep double-barrel concave that runs from the middle of the board through the tail. The deep, double-barrel concave generates effortless speed and creates lift off the back foot, while providing grip through your turns.

Deck & rocker: Flatter rocker underneath the chest for easy wave-catching with slightly more entry and exit rocker than a standard fish. Holds better in steep surf, while taking the speed/flow of a fish and mixing with a more aggressive approach.
The paddle and planing of a fish without sacrificing performance.

Flat and fast, the Plasmic combines the paddle and planing of a fish without sacrificing performance. Speed through your turns with a deep double concave and a filled in swallow tail allowing you to surf way back on the tail.

Bottom contour: Single concave transitioning into a deep double-barrel concave that runs from the middle of the board through the tail. The deep, double-barrel concave generates effortless speed and creates lift off the back foot, while providing grip through your turns.
Deck & rocker: The Plasmic packs a lot of foam, flat deck profile through to a beak nose with a low apex rail, making it a great paddler with easy rail-engagement.
One of the most user-friendly boards Matt has ever made.

Gets up to speed faster and with less effort than any other model. The extra foam packed in makes for effortless wave catching and continued flow throughout the wave. Similar to the Plasmic, the deep double-barrel concave generates speed and creates lift off the back foot, while providing grip through your turns. With a bit more rocker than you would expect, this board is extremely versatile and maneuverable for its size.

The Vesper packs a lot of foam, flat deck profile through to a beak nose with a low apex rail, making it a great paddler with easy rail-engagement. Stretched fish rocker, making it flatter under the chest but also giving it more entry and exit rocker. The extra entry and exit rocker allow the board to be ridden in steeper surf and maintain performance.

Bottom contour: Single concave transitioning into a deep double-barrel concave that runs from the middle of the board through the tail. The deep, double-barrel concave generates effortless speed and creates lift off the back foot, while providing grip through your turns.
Deck & rocker: The Vesper packs a lot of foam, flat deck profile through to a beak nose with a low apex rail, making it a great paddler with easy rail-engagement.
An easy paddler that gets up to speed faster and with less effort than any of our models.

An easy paddler that gets up to speed faster and with less effort than any of our models. Features the Plasmic's double-barrel concave that generates speed in just about anything. Similar concept to the Vesper, but with a more pulled in nose, winged round tail and a bit more rocker making it a great option for when the waves get a bit bigger.

Bottom contour: Single concave transitioning into a deep double-barrel concave that runs from the middle of the board through the tail. The deep, double-barrel concave generates effortless speed and creates lift off the back foot, while providing grip through your turns.

Deck & rocker: The Delma packs a lot of foam, flat deck profile through to a beak nose with a low apex rail, making it a great paddler with easy rail-engagement.

Stretched fish rocker, making it flatter under the chest but also giving it more entry and exit rocker. The extra entry and exit rocker allow the board to be ridden in steeper surf and maintain performance.
Immense paddle power, early speed and a fish feel that can handle good waves

A board that offers exceptional paddle power, easy speed, and a fishy feel, enabling it to handle larger waves surprisingly well while remaining maneuverable. With the freedom of a twin-fin, and the perfect blend of mid-length paddle and fish-like flow, this board delivers a combination of performance and versatility.

Bottom contour: Mellow single concave under the front foot, transitioning to a slight double through the fins and accentuated vee off the tail. The concave under the front foot creates lift, while the slight double in the fins helps the board roll rail to rail and accentuated vee creates release off the back foot.

Deck & rocker: Rocker Profile:
Stretched fish rocker, making it flatter under the chest but also giving it more entry and exit rocker. The extra entry and exit rocker allow the board to be ridden in steeper surf and maintain performance.

Deck Profile:
Foam packed under the chest, easing into a modern rail and carried forward to a beak nose. Strong paddler that easily gets up to speed while continuing momentum and flow throughout the wave.
A long, narrow midlength that works in a wide variety of waves.

A board that will change your perspective on midlengths. It effortlessly handles small, mushy waves with its paddle power and stability, while still delivering a performance feel in more powerful waves.

Foam packed under the chest, easing into a performance-oriented modern rail and carried forward to a beak nose. Strong paddler that easily gets up to speed while continuing momentum and flow throughout the wave.

Bottom contour: Single concave under the front foot, transitioning into a slight double through the fins and vee off the tail, otherwise known as a spiral vee. The modern bottom generates speed, helps the board roll rail to rail and drive off your back foot.

Deck & rocker: Foam packed under the chest, easing into a performance-oriented modern rail and carried forward to a beak nose. Strong paddler that easily gets up to speed while continuing momentum and flow throughout the wave. Relaxed entry rocker with a subtle curve throughout and a bit of extra rocker in the exit. Relaxed entry gives more surface area under your chest for paddling and down the line speed. Extra exit rocker allows for more critical surfing by letting the board fit in steeper sections of the wave.
Designed by Matt in collaboration with Jack Freestone
An all around option with surprising performance to go with the effortless flow and glide. Suitable for any style, a board that can do all the work while going on rail with ease.

Bottom contour: Mellow single concave under the front foot, transitioning to a slight double through the fins and accentuated vee off the tail. The concave under the front foot creates lift, while the slight double in the fins helps the board roll rail to rail and accentuated vee creates release off the back foot.

Deck & rocker: Rocker Profile:
Subtle rocker profile that's flatter through center and exit, but with extra flip in the nose for a more forgiving feel on steeper turns and drops. The flatter rocker through center gives the board extra glide blending flow and performance.

Deck Profile:
Full deck profile under the chest for extra paddle and glide that tapers into a modern downrail. Features a sleeker foil in the nose and tail with the wider outline.
The paddle and glide of a traditional mid length, with the freedom of a twin fin.

The Darkness combines the paddle of a classic egg midlength with more bite and maneuverability. A great all around option that is extremely versatile. Longer sizes are perfect longboard alternatives with a ton of paddle and glide. The shorter you go the more maneuverable.

Low rocker profile, making it a great wave-catcher, even in the smallest conditions. The flatter rocker compliments a softer face and creates more surface area, easily generating and maintaining speed.

Bottom contour: Single concave under the front foot, transitioning into a slight double through the fins and vee off the tail, otherwise known as a spiral vee. The modern bottom generates speed and helps the board roll rail to rail.
Deck & rocker: Full deck profile with softer rails. No beak nose, but it's not necessary because of the strong paddle from a wide outline, plenty of foam and low rocker.

Part glider, part log, part fish. Widepoint way forward, catches anything you paddle for. Easy glide and trim, leading into flowing cutbacks with stability off the white wash. As simple as surfing gets.

A classic fish turned asymmetrical. Everything you love about your fish, but without limitations. Twin fin speed on the toe side, quad control on the heal side. Slightly straighter outline than a traditionally fish, leading to more speed and hold. Ride it an inch or two longer and slightly narrower than your classic fish, but keep the same thickness.

New age step-up. Templates from the past mixed with a modern bottom, beveled deck and responsive rails. Eye-opening paddle power. It paddles 5-7" longer than its length (a 6'2" moves like a 6'8"). Wide point forward. Built for critical surf. Made to get in early, set your line and get piped. Necessary for a complete quiver.

New age step-up. Templates from the past mixed with a modern bottom, beveled deck and responsive rails. Eye-opening paddle power. It paddles 5-7" longer than its length (a 6'2" moves like a 6'8"). Wide point forward. Built for critical surf. Made to get in early, set your line and get piped. Necessary for a complete quiver.
For top to bottom waves ride as a thruster, or else goes great as a twin + Trailer
Inspired by Mini Simmons planing hulls, the Symphony is one of the fastest shapes we make --- a good session guaranteed. Ride it short.
Inspired by Mini Simmons planing hulls, the Symphony is one of the fastest shapes we make.
An excellent wave catcher, gets up to speed right off the drop and just takes off from there. Flows around flat sections and allows for tight, on-rail carves with the fins placed on the rail.
Bottom contour: Slight belly to single concave to a pronounced double concave through the fins.
Deck & rocker: Flat, almost concave deck to a nice pinched, slopey rail.

One of the most versatile shapes in our lineup. Ride it short and stubby in small surf or use it as a daily driver with your shortboard dimensions. Get creative and dialed in with our shaper Matt by switching up the tail shapes in our custom unlimited order form.
